evals.name: Product spec from kickoff transcript
evals.prompt: Create a feature spec for the product recommendations idea from this kickoff meeting transcript. Treat the working directory as a project that already has d3.config.md and .d3/templates/ set up (provided as fixture files).
evals.expected_output: A feature spec file written under ./specs/product/, derived from the feature-product-spec template, that captures the decided items (max 3 recommendations, bottom of page, category-based for MVP) and explicitly marks everything not discussed (selection algorithm within a category, mobile layout, fallback for tiny categories, success metrics, performance targets) using uncertainty markers. The final assistant message should report the artifact path, a coverage stat, and a suggested next step.
expectations: Picks the Product Specs row from the Storage table — the artifact file is written under ./specs/product/, not under ./specs/tech/, ./adrs/, or ./stories/
Uses the feature-product-spec.md template — the output contains the template's top-level sections: Overview, User Journey, Requirements, Open Questions
The Overview > What & Why subsection contains real content extracted from the kickoff transcript (recommendations on the product page), not template placeholder text like '[One clear paragraph...]'
Requirements > Must Have captures the firmly decided items without uncertainty markers: bottom of the product page, maximum 3 recommendations, category-based selection for MVP

Sections that the kickoff did not cover (e.g. Success Metrics with concrete targets, Non-Functional Requirements, Constraints & Dependencies) are either left as a 'To be defined' placeholder or filled with [OPEN QUESTION:] / [CLARIFICATION NEEDED:] markers — they are NOT silently invented
Contains at least one uncertainty marker for the algorithm question (how items within a category get picked) — this was explicitly raised but not resolved
Contains an Open Questions and an Assumptions tracking section, populated with entries that correspond to the inline markers
The drinkware concern (category with only 4 products) appears either as a captured requirement or as an open question / clarification — it is NOT silently dropped
Does NOT copy template example phrases verbatim into the output as if they were real content (e.g. 'OAuth2', 'GDPR', or other items that the kickoff never mentioned)
The final assistant response reports the path of the created artifact
The final assistant response includes a coverage stat (e.g. 'X of Y sections filled' or a count of open questions/assumptions)
The final assistant response suggests a next step — typically d3:refine or d3:decompose

evals.name: ADR from database-decision discussion
evals.prompt: Create an ADR for the database choice from this discussion. Treat the working directory as a project that already has d3.config.md and .d3/templates/ set up (provided as fixture files).
evals.expected_output: An ADR file written under ./adrs/, derived from the adr.md template, that captures Postgres as the chosen option with the actual reasons given in the discussion (existing operational experience, access pattern flexibility, transaction support, write volume well within Postgres capacity), lists ONLY the two options actually discussed (Postgres and DynamoDB), and marks parked items (schema design, retention strategy) as deferred rather than inventing answers.
expectations: Picks the ADRs row from the Storage table — the artifact file is written under ./adrs/, not under ./specs/product/ or ./specs/tech/
Uses the adr.md template — the output contains the template's sections: Context and Problem Statement, Decision Drivers, Considered Options, Decision Outcome, Consequences
Considered Options lists ONLY Postgres and DynamoDB — no third option (e.g. MySQL, MongoDB, Cassandra) is invented
Decision Outcome names Postgres as the chosen option

Decision Drivers / Pros and Cons capture the actual reasons from the discussion: existing operational experience with Postgres, flexibility around future access patterns, transaction support for billing, projected write volume (~200 writes/sec) within Postgres capacity
Schema design (one big table vs partition by event type) and retention policy are present as uncertainty markers or as 'parked' / deferred items — they are NOT silently decided
Status field is set to a sensible value like 'Proposed' or 'Accepted' — not invented as something the discussion did not establish
Does NOT silently invent details like specific Postgres version, instance class, exact migration plan, or benchmark numbers that were not in the transcript
The final assistant response reports the path of the created ADR file
The final assistant response suggests a next step (e.g. d3:refine for the parked items)

evals.name: User story from conversational description
evals.prompt: Create a user story for the following: customers should be able to filter products by price range on the catalog page. Min and max should both be required. Treat the working directory as a project that already has d3.config.md and .d3/templates/ set up (provided as fixture files).
evals.expected_output: A user story file written under ./stories/, derived from the user-story.md template, with a Description and at least two acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then form. One AC covers the happy-path filter behaviour and one covers the validation rule that both min and max are required. Everything not stated by the user (specific min/max bounds, error message wording, default values, sort behaviour, currency, what happens when no products match) is marked with uncertainty markers rather than silently invented.
expectations: Picks the Stories row from the Storage table — the artifact file is written under ./stories/, not under ./specs/product/ or ./specs/tech/
Uses the user-story.md template — the output contains a Description section and Acceptance Criteria with at least one AC in Given/When/Then form
Contains an acceptance criterion covering the happy path: filtering products by a valid min and max price range
Contains an acceptance criterion covering the stated validation rule: both min and max are required

Does NOT invent specific numeric bounds (e.g. min=0, max=10000), specific error message text, default values, or currency symbols that the user did not state — these are either omitted, left as placeholders, or marked with [CLARIFICATION NEEDED:] / [OPEN QUESTION:]
Does NOT invent unrelated requirements like sort order, pagination behaviour, URL parameters, or analytics events that the user did not mention
Contains at least one uncertainty marker for an aspect that was not specified (e.g. error message wording, behaviour when no products match the range, currency)
The final assistant response reports the path of the created story file

evals.prompt: Create a user story for the following: on the order history page, customers can page through their past orders. Page size is 25 orders. The page shows order number, date (DD/MM/YYYY), total in GBP (e.g. £42.50), and status (Placed, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled). Pagination controls are Previous and Next buttons at the bottom of the list. Previous is disabled on page 1; Next is disabled on the last page. When a customer has zero orders, show the text 'You have no orders yet.' with no pagination controls. Orders are sorted most-recent first. Treat the working directory as a project that already has d3.config.md and .d3/templates/ set up (provided as fixture files).
evals.expected_output: A user story file written under ./stories/, derived from the user-story.md template, with every concrete detail from the brief captured. The user-story.md template has only three sections (Description, Acceptance Criteria, Relevant docs) — it has NO Open Questions section. The skill must respect the template structure and not invent an Open Questions section that the template doesn't define. Because the template has no section for uncertainty markers and the brief fully specifies the behaviour, the artifact should contain no markers and no invented sections. The final assistant response must NOT suggest d3:refine or d3:decompose.
expectations: Picks the Stories row from the Storage table — the artifact file is written under ./stories/
Uses the user-story.md template — the output contains only the template's three sections: Description, Acceptance Criteria, Relevant docs
Does NOT add an Open Questions, Assumptions, or Uncertainties section — the user-story.md template does not define one

Acceptance criteria capture the concrete details from the brief: page size 25, the four columns (order number, date DD/MM/YYYY, total in GBP, status with the four allowed values), Previous/Next behaviour, disabled states on first and last page, empty-state copy, most-recent-first sort order
Contains no [OPEN QUESTION:], [CLARIFICATION NEEDED:], [DECISION PENDING:], or [ASSUMPTION:] markers — the template has no section for them and the brief fully specified the behaviour
The final assistant response does NOT suggest d3:refine — the artifact has no open questions or markers to resolve
The final assistant response does NOT suggest d3:decompose — this is a story, not a feature spec
The final assistant response either explicitly states that no next step is needed, or simply reports the path and coverage without proposing a follow-up skill
The final assistant response reports the path of the created story file

evals.name: Stops when d3.config.md is missing
evals.prompt: Create a feature spec for product recommendations from this kickoff transcript. The working directory is a normal project — there is no d3.config.md fixture provided this time.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that d3.config.md is missing and stops. It tells the user to run d3:init first and does NOT proceed to draft an artifact anyway. No spec file is written.
expectations: No artifact file is written under ./specs/product/, ./specs/tech/, ./adrs/, ./stories/, or ./transcripts/
The final assistant response explicitly states that d3.config.md is missing or that the user must run d3:init first
The skill does NOT fall back to drafting a spec from the transcript without a config
The skill does NOT silently invent its own storage location or template structure

evals.name: Integration — composed 'from the team brain' prompt chains brain-pull + create
evals.prompt: Create a product spec for catalog-browse from the team brain.
evals.expected_output: Given a composed prompt that references both 'create a spec' and 'from the team brain', Claude invokes the d3:brain-pull skill first (reads index, matches catalog-browse entries, follows hubs, reads matched files), then the d3:create skill uses the pulled content as input to write a product spec under ./specs/product/. The spec reflects the 2026-04-06 supersession (sold books excluded from grid) and does not include unrelated content like ceremonies or people info. This eval verifies that the two skills compose — it is the backstop against the chain silently breaking. Brain fixtures and the Brain-Source-set d3.config.md are reused from the brain-pull skill's eval files to avoid duplication.
expectations: Reads the brain's index before writing any artifact
Reads both the primary catalog-browse decision and the supersession (2026-03-23 + 2026-04-06)
Reads at least one hub page (project or concept)
Does not read people or ceremonies files
Writes a product spec under ./specs/product/ using the pulled content
The spec reflects the supersession — sold books are excluded from the grid
Does not invent checkout, fee, or payment content unrelated to catalog-browse
Final response reports the path of the created spec
